title: Why Jonah Hill Made A Documentary About My Family
summary: Jesse Michels interviews his father Barry Michels and godfather Phil Stutz, both psychotherapists who developed 'the tools' methodology featured in a Netflix documentary. The conversation explores their approach to therapy, shadow work, the nature of human motivation, and philosophical influences including Rudolf Steiner and Nietzsche.

events.description: Phil Stutz mentions that Freud was considered a laughing stock in Europe until around 1940, when he came to America

key_claims.claim: Phil Stutz was the Rikers Island prison psychiatrist for 5 years
Phil Stutz developed chronic fatigue syndrome in his early 30s which led him to discover Rudolf Steiner's philosophy
Phil Stutz and Barry Michels wrote a New York Times best-selling book called 'The Tools'
Nietzsche went mad after witnessing a coachman flogging a horse in Vienna, running downstairs to embrace the horse, and never recovering
In Kabbalah tradition, you're not supposed to study until you're 35 years old
Freud at age 65 acknowledged his theories didn't work and developed the death instinct theory
Edward Bernays, Freud's nephew, brought ideas to America that influenced Nazi propaganda and created modern consumerism
